result.summary: PostgreSQL delivers 2-5x better single-node OLTP latency and throughput than CockroachDB, but CockroachDB provides linear horizontal scalability and automatic geo-distribution, making it superior for multi-region workloads requiring strong consistency above 50K TPS.
key_findings: Single-node PostgreSQL achieves 2.3x higher TPC-C throughput (45K tpmC vs 19K tpmC) with P99 latencies of 12ms vs 34ms on equivalent hardware
CockroachDB's distributed transactions add 15-25ms overhead per cross-range operation but maintain linearizable consistency across regions without application-level sharding
Break-even point for CockroachDB occurs at approximately 3 nodes: beyond this, its horizontal scaling advantage surpasses PostgreSQL's vertical scaling ceiling on single-node configurations
